content.problemStatement:
In this worked-out example, we'll create a simple personal blog template using Flask and Jinja2 templating. 

We'll establish a base template and create two pages that extend from it: a home page and an about page. \
This will demonstrate how to use base templates to maintain a consistent layout across different pages of a website.

### How does this code work
1. When you run the code, the Flask application loads `base.html` \
`base.html` currently has the Header, Footer and the Nav bar
2. When we click on 'Home', the Flask application routes us to `home.html`. \
`home.html` is the child template that extends `base.html`. \
It overwrites the `{% block content %}` in `base.html` - hence we see description of the blog


3. When we click on 'About', the Flask application routes us to `about.html`. \
`about.html` is the child template that extends `base.html`. \
It overwrites the `{% block content %}` in `base.html` - hence we see details about the creator of the blog.
